label: PASS
rationale: Hardware reset is reset type 1, and the accepted LockOnReset value [0,1] includes that reset type; the Core LockOnReset rule/state machine therefore requires ReadLocked and WriteLocked to become True while preserving the LockOnReset value.

label: FAIL
rationale: After accepting LockOnReset [0,1], a hardware reset matches reset type 1, so ReadLocked and WriteLocked should be set True on reset recovery; returning both as False is inconsistent with the reset-locking rule.

label: PASS
rationale: LockOnReset [0,3] contains Power Cycle and Programmatic, not Hardware reset type 1. The nonmatching reset path preserves the prior ReadLocked and WriteLocked values, which were both False.

label: FAIL
rationale: Hardware reset type 1 does not match LockOnReset [0,3], so the range should recover with the same False ReadLocked and WriteLocked values it had before reset; returning both True incorrectly applies a matching-reset lock.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was set True, enabling TPER_RESET; Opal requires an enabled TPER_RESET to set ReadLocked and WriteLocked True for Locking objects whose LockOnReset contains Programmatic, which [0,3] does.

label: FAIL
rationale: With ProgrammaticResetEnable True and LockOnReset [0,3], an enabled TPER_RESET must set ReadLocked and WriteLocked True; returning both False violates the Opal TPER_RESET requirement.

label: PASS
rationale: An empty LockOnReset set means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset. Since both were True before the power cycle, returning both True is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The empty LockOnReset set preserves the current lock state through resets. The prior ReadLocked and WriteLocked values were True, so returning False for both after a power cycle is unsupported.

label: PASS
rationale: With LockOnReset empty, reset events do not change ReadLocked or WriteLocked. Both values were False before the hardware reset, so the returned False values are compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: An empty LockOnReset set preserves the existing lock bits through reset. Because ReadLocked and WriteLocked were False before the hardware reset, returning both True incorrectly changes state.

label: PASS
rationale: WriteLockEnabled was False, so the write-locking feature is disabled and WriteLocked is ignored even though LockOnReset [0] leaves/sets the lock bit True after power cycle. A user-data write to the range may succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because WriteLockEnabled is False, WriteLocked is ignored and the write-locking feature is disabled; rejecting the post-power-cycle write solely due to the True lock bit is not supported by the cited locking-column rules.

label: FAIL
rationale: Although LockOnReset [0] would set WriteLocked True on a power cycle, the power cycle occurred while the Locking SP session was still open, and Core requires power cycles to abort all open sessions. A subsequent Get succeeding without a new StartSession is therefore not protocol-compliant.
